---
format: html
title: filter_by_n_samples()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>filter_by_n_samples</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Filter Taxa by Minimum Number of Spatio-Temporal Samples</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Filters out taxa that are not present in a sufficient number of
spatio-temporal samples (distinct dataset-age combinations). Only
taxa occurring in at least 'min_n_samples' distinct
'(dataset_name, age)' combinations are retained. This removes
taxa that are present in too few interpolated time steps to
provide reliable co-occurrence signal.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>filter_by_n_samples(data = NULL, min_n_samples = 1)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data">data</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame containing community data in long format. Must include
columns 'taxon', 'dataset_name', and 'age'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="min_n_samples">min_n_samples</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single positive integer specifying the minimum number of distinct
spatio-temporal samples (dataset-age combinations) a taxon must
appear in to be retained. Default is 1 (no filtering).</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>The function counts distinct '(dataset_name, age)' combinations per
'taxon'. Taxa with fewer combinations than 'min_n_samples' are
removed. An error is raised if no taxa remain after filtering, which
may indicate that 'min_n_samples' is set too high.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A filtered data frame containing only taxa that appear in at least
'min_n_samples' distinct spatio-temporal samples. All original
columns are preserved.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[filter_community_by_n_cores()], [filter_rare_taxa()],
[select_n_taxa()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
